title: Information Erasure Observatory
thesis: The field measures access in the present tense. We measure erasure across time — what was removed or rewritten — on three layers, and we seal our own record so it cannot be quietly rewritten in turn.
index_scale: 0-100; higher = more of the observed record removed or rewritten

layers.title: Network erasure
layers.detail: reachability that disappeared — sites, messengers and tools blocked at the wire

layers.title: Model erasure
layers.detail: answers a state-aligned model will no longer give — refusals and party-line substitution

layers.title: Narrative erasure
layers.detail: encyclopedia entries rewritten to the state line — sensitive terms excised, sourcing collapsed to state media
layers.reason: live network not enabled (PALIMPSEST_LIVE / PALIMPSEST_PROXY unset)

cross_checks.name: Censored Planet interference
cross_checks.note: independent DNS/HTTP side-channel, confirms network layer

integrity.note: every source reading above is hash-chained into the ledger at capture time; recompute the chain yourself to prove no past reading was altered or dropped

vs_access_indices: Access observatories answer 'is X reachable now'. This answers 'what did the record lose, and can you prove our measurement of the loss was not itself edited afterward'.
